solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def lastSubstring(self, s: str) -> str:
        n = len(s)
        max_index = 0
        for i in range(1, n):
            j, k = i, 0
            while j < n and s[max_index + k] < s[j]:
                k += 1
                j += 1
            if j < n and s[max_index + k] == s[j]:
                max_index = max_index if max_index > i else i
        return s[max_index:]
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: This solution iterates through the string and compares substrings starting at each index to find the lexicographically maximum substring. It uses a two-pointer technique to compare characters and update the index of the maximum substring. The time complexity is O(n^2) in the worst case, which may not be optimal for large inputs.
